title: Leucanthemum monspeliense
intro: Leucanthemum monspeliense, also known as Montpellier daisy or Pyrenean daisy, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum monspeliense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum monspeliense is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among other flowering plants that produce showy blooms and small, composite flowers. The genus Leucanthemum, to which L. monspeliense belongs, includes several species of daisies native to the Mediterranean region. L. monspeliense is a member of the tribe Anthemideae, a group of plants characterized by their daisy-like flowers and often silvery-gray foliage. As a member of the Asteraceae family, L. monspeliense shares common characteristics with other plants in this diverse and widespread family.
uses: Leucanthemum monspeliense, also known as Montpellier daisy, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In some regions, the leaves and flowers of Leucanthemum monspeliense are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and fever. benefits: Leucanthemum monspeliense, also known as Pyrenean oxeye daisy, is believed to have various health benefits. properties: Leucanthemum monspeliense, also known as the French daisy, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its therapeutic properties.
